statement.en: Biological age is a concept, not a construct : assessments of healthy aging using epigenetic clocks, physiological, and functional measures of aging
statement.summary: The full abstract for this thesis is available in the body of the thesis, and will be available when the embargo expires.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The thesis presents a provisional distinction between biological age as 'concept' vs 'construct' with three measurement modalities (epigenetic, physiological, functional). This is preprint/unpublished work (embargo status) with empirical claims not yet peer-validated. The core proposition—that biological age has ontological primacy over social construction—is actively debated in gerontology and requires validation against competing frameworks.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 時間の経過は、身体の変化の累積に等しくない。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 同心円の層と、その中を流れる非線形の時間——見かけの年齢と真の老化の不可解な乖離。
